extractor_tasks.task: Perform an initial scan to identify the contract type: content licensing agreement, talent/performance agreement, music synchronization license, distribution agreement, digital rights/UGC license, or hybrid/other.
Extract the document header, title, and any template or disclaimer notices.
Identify all contracting parties, their defined short names (e.g., "Licensor", "Performer"), organizational types, jurisdictions, and principal office locations.
Extract all recitals (WHEREAS clauses) establishing the purpose and background of the agreement.

Identify and catalog every defined term in the agreement. Defined terms are typically capitalized and introduced with quotation marks or parenthetical definitions. Extract each term and its full definition verbatim.

Extract the complete grant of rights: Nature of the grant (exclusive, non-exclusive, non-transferable) Specific rights granted (reproduce, distribute, perform, display, synchronize, make available, exploit) Licensed media and platforms with all enumerated categories Territory specifications Language, dubbing, and subtitle rights — which languages are authorized, who bears localization costs, who approves quality, and who owns the localized versions Sublicensing and assignment restrictions Reservation of rights language Holdback provisions and exclusivity restrictions

Extract ownership and chain of title information: Which party is identified as the underlying IP owner (copyright, trademark) Any chain-of-title warranty language Work-for-hire classifications or assignment documentation referenced Any referenced prior encumbrances, liens, or existing licenses on the IP Any known title disputes or pending claims mentioned in the agreement

Extract all financial and compensation terms: Fixed fees, license fees, and synchronization fees with amounts or blank indicators Revenue share percentages and the definition of the revenue base (Net Revenue, Gross Receipts, Net Profits) Minimum guarantees and recoupment mechanics Residuals and royalty structures including guild references Distribution fees and commission rates Recoupable expense categories Payment schedules, thresholds, and reporting periods Most-favored-nations provisions Per diem, travel, and production allowances Contingent compensation triggers

Extract term and termination provisions: Initial term duration and commencement trigger Renewal mechanics, notice periods, and automatic renewal conditions Termination triggers (breach, force majeure, incapacity, abandonment) Cure periods and notice requirements Post-termination obligations (cease exploitation, return/destroy materials, final accounting) Reversion of rights provisions Sell-off periods for existing commitments Survival clauses identifying which provisions outlast termination

Extract all obligation and restriction provisions: Delivery requirements and technical specifications Exclusivity periods and appearance/alteration restrictions Morals clauses Key person clauses — identify the key person and consequences of unavailability Editorial control rights — who may cut, edit, re-version, reformat, and whether approval is required Approval and consultation rights (marketing, key art, credit, trailers) Reporting, accounting, and audit rights with frequency and notice periods Content standards and community guidelines compliance DMCA and takedown compliance obligations Content moderation rights and appeal processes Data portability and analytics access provisions Publicity and promotional obligations

Extract talent and third-party obligation provisions: Guild and union references (SAG-AFTRA, WGA, DGA, IATSE) and compliance obligations Residual and royalty obligations to performers, writers, directors — who bears responsibility and under which CBA Music licensing status: sync rights clearance, master use license status, public performance rights and PRO affiliations (ASCAP, BMI, SESAC, GMR) Embedded IP clearances: third-party logos, footage, artwork, brand marks, archival material — whether cleared and by whom Any third-party consents still required or identified as pending
Extract credit and attribution provisions: screen credit specifications, paid advertising obligations, music credit requirements, and credit remedy limitations.

Extract all legal protection provisions: Representations and warranties by each party Indemnification obligations (mutual or asymmetric) Limitation of liability and damage exclusions Insurance requirements: E&O coverage minimums, general liability, whether the other party must be named as additional insured, proof-of-coverage deadlines Governing law and jurisdiction Dispute resolution mechanism (arbitration, mediation, litigation) Force majeure provisions with enumerated triggering events Entire agreement, amendment, and waiver clauses

Extract all digital and emerging rights provisions: AI training rights — restrictions or grants on using content, performances, or likenesses as ML training data AI-generated content — rights to create new content using AI based on licensed material Digital likeness and deepfake protections — consent requirements, per-use authorization, additional compensation triggers NFT, metaverse, and interactive media rights — whether addressed, reserved, or granted Social media clip rights — authorized duration, platforms, and whether clips may be independently monetized Viewer data ownership — which party owns audience data, sharing obligations, anonymization requirements Data privacy compliance — specific regulatory regimes referenced (GDPR, CCPA, etc.) and compliance obligations

Extract regulatory and compliance provisions: Content rating and classification obligations Local content quotas (EU AVMS Directive, Australian/Canadian requirements) Accessibility requirements — closed captioning, audio description, subtitle standards Export controls and sanctions compliance — restricted territories, OFAC/EU sanctions references Data protection law compliance — which regimes apply and how obligations are allocated
Extract all references to exhibits, schedules, and attachments, noting their described contents even if the exhibits themselves are not present in the document.
Note all signature blocks, including party names, signatory titles, and date fields.

Populate extraction_notes: List every blank fill-in field with its section reference Note any truncated text at page boundaries Catalog all cross-references to other sections, exhibits, or external documents Note any defined terms used but not defined within the visible document
Populate metadata: contract type, document title, completeness, document status, blank field count, defined term count.
Run the verification process defined in extractor_verification.xml before finalizing output.
If no media rights contract content is found, respond with the not_found_response as specified in extractor_verification.xml.
